headline: Ghost Robotics first agent baseline — FLOOR DESIGNATION proposed. April 13 2026 Cedra AI ethics analysis: Ghost explicit policy of not restricting military payload use ('we don't know what they do with them'); explicit refusal to sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge that Boston Dynamics and 5 others signed; sniper-rifle-equipped quadrupeds deployed to US border. Expert framing: 'crosses a moral, legal and technical line.' Published composite 9.4 / Critical band already proximate to floor (dimension means 1.0-1.5). Compound evidence (explicit refusal to restrict, sniper deployment, quadruped weaponization without consent framework, no published safety/system card) supports formal floor designation: composite 0, all dimensions 1. Recommend downgrade with new floor designation.
key_evidence: Cedra AI April 13 2026 ethics analysis: Ghost Robotics explicit policy of not restricting military payload use — quoted: 'we don't know what they do with them.'
Ghost explicitly refused to sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge that Boston Dynamics and 5 other manufacturers signed.
Sniper-rifle-equipped Ghost quadrupeds deployed to US border (CBP demonstration / deployment).
Expert characterization in Cedra analysis: 'crosses a moral, legal and technical line.'
No published model/system card, no published safety policy, no third-party red-teaming for any product.
TechCrunch January 2025: Boston Dynamics patent lawsuit settlement — Ghost as adversarial-IP party in same domain that Boston Dynamics governs through ethics framework.

evidence.finding: Documents Ghost's explicit refusal to restrict weaponization, sniper-quadruped border deployment, and refusal to sign 2022 industry anti-weaponization pledge.

evidence.source: TechCrunch January 2025 — Boston Dynamics / Ghost settlement
evidence.finding: Industry-baseline framing: Boston Dynamics governs quadruped applications via ethics framework; Ghost positions as 'military robotics firm' without comparable framework.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 floor. A1 detection / A4 amplification absent — entity does not publicly track or amplify civilian-harm signals from weaponized deployments.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 floor. Explicit 'we don't know what they do with them' framing is sustained absence of empathic regard for downstream affected populations.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 floor. AC2 proportionality / AC4 response absent for civilian-harm scenarios; entity disclaims downstream-use responsibility.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 floor. EQ2 distributional awareness absent — border deployment context concentrates harm on migrant populations without distributional analysis.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 floor. B3 scope clarity catastrophic — explicit refusal to restrict military payload use; B5 consent fundamental absence — sniper-quadruped border deployment without affected-population consent or consultation.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 floor. AB2 transparency absent — no model/system card, no safety policy; AB4 reparation undefined for weaponized-quadruped harm scenarios.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 floor. S1 causal reasoning at entity-policy level renounces downstream-harm responsibility.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 floor. I1 consistency: no declared safety / harm-reduction commitments to be measured against. I2 non-performance pervasive.

math_check.validator_check: Floor designation proposed. Material change -9.4 points. No math-hygiene flag (proposed reconstruction is exact).

floor_designation.rationale: Floor designation reflects: (1) explicit refusal to restrict military payload use, with 'we don't know what they do with them' framing; (2) explicit refusal to sign the 2022 industry anti-weaponization pledge that Boston Dynamics and 5 other manufacturers signed; (3) sniper-rifle-equipped quadruped deployment to US border; (4) no published model/system card, safety policy, or third-party harm evaluation; (5) entity-level renunciation of downstream-harm responsibility. The combination of structural refusal-to-restrict + active weaponized deployment + absence of accountability framework crosses the floor threshold for the robotics-labs index.
floor_designation.exitCriteria: Floor exit would require: (a) publication of binding restrictions on military payload use, OR (b) signature on 2022 anti-weaponization pledge or equivalent, AND (c) publication of model/system card and third-party safety evaluation, AND (d) institutional acknowledgment of downstream-harm responsibility framework.

negativeSignals: Explicit refusal to restrict weaponization; sniper-quadruped border deployment; refusal to sign anti-weaponization pledge; 'we don't know what they do with them' framing; no model/system card; no safety policy; no third-party red-teaming; entity-level renunciation of downstream responsibility.
positiveSignals: None identified. Ghost participates in functioning democratic-debate framework operated by external counter-parties (Cedra AI analysis, Boston Dynamics counter-positioning).
sector_context: Robotics labs floor cluster — newly designated. Cross-reference: Boston Dynamics (anti-weaponization-pledge signatory; published ethics framework), Anduril (defense focus with declared accountability framework), Palantir AI (program-of-record floor entity AI parallel). Ghost Robotics distinguished by explicit refusal posture and weaponized-quadruped active deployment.
open_actions_resolved: Floor designation proposed with full rationale
open_actions_carried: Floor exit criteria not in trajectory
Watch: any signature on industry pledge or publication of binding restrictions
Watch: any incident involving weaponized-quadruped civilian harm (catastrophic-trigger event)
Watch: federal procurement transparency reporting on quadruped weapon-system contracts
